_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MCh/ or equivalent (in Cardiothoracic Surgery, Vascular Surgery)
DM/DNB/Equivalent in Cardiology or Interventional Radiology
clinical_indications: Congenital fistulas are typically repaired when they present due to sequelae (pathological consequences) in later life, as many may remain asymptomatic until adulthood.
Hemodynamic instability, injury to adjacent tissue, and unsuccessful endovascular repair are all indications for open surgical intervention on a fistula.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with detailed history, indications for the procedure and admission notes
Relevant investigations - Duplex ultrasound scan/Angiography/CTAngiography (CTA)/ MRA

claim.label: Barcode of the implant or graft if used (optional)

common_queries: History of trauma or intervention that may cause arteriovenous fistula was not ruled out for the patient.
Patient's investigation reports (Duplex ultrasound scan/Angiography/CTAngiography/MRA) were not suggestive of the procedure.
Clinical notes with detailed history, indications for the procedure, or admission notes were not submitted at pre-authorization.
Relevant investigation reports (Duplex ultrasound scan/Angiography/CTAngiography/MRA) were not submitted at pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) or detailed procedure/operative notes were not submitted at claim submission.
Barcode of the implant or graft was not submitted at claim submission, if an implant or graft was used.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Tertiary Care Facilities with the availability of Cath Lab.
clinical_key_pointers: An abnormal connection between an artery and a vein is known as an arteriovenous fistula (AVF). Congenital Arterio Venous Fistula occurs in isolation or as a part of a complex arteriovenous (AV) malformation.
surgical_options: Repair of arteriovenous fistula (AVF) can be done by endovascular or open surgical procedures
Endovascular management includes the use of coils, stent-grafts, covered stents and cyanoacrylate glue for closing the fistula.

Open Surgical Repair can be made via autogenous (usually saphenous vein) grafts, synthetic grafts, venous ligation (diameter dependent), bypass, or complex reconstruction involving one or more of the above.
